column1: # PURPOSE: Synthetic edge (recommendation) data for automated testing.
# This file mimics the real "vis_channel_recs2.csv" from the Recfluence dataset.
# Each row represents one directed recommendation from one channel to another.
# "Directed" means the edge has a direction: FROM recommends TO
# necessarily the reverse. Think of it like a one-way street.
# COLUMN DEFINITIONS (matching the real data exactly):
# FROM_CHANNEL_ID     — The source channel. YouTube recommended content FROM
# TO_CHANNEL_ID       — The target channel. ...TO this channel.

column2: FROM→TO forms a directed edge in the graph.

#                     — What fraction of the source channel's total outgoing

#                       ch_L1 point to this particular target.
#                       All outgoing percentages from one source channel
#    ch_R1 → ch_R1. A channel recommending itself. The real data has 6
#    of these. Our graph_builder must filter them out because a self-loop
#    doesn't represent a meaningful recommendation — it's YouTube
#    recommending more of the same channel you're already watching.
#    After filtering: 16 total rows - 1 self-loop = 15 valid edges.
#    Left channels recommending other Left channels. This creates a small
#    echo chamber — once you enter the Left cluster
#    you there. Tests can verify assortative mixing (like-recommends-like).
#    Right channels forming a cycle. A random walk starting at ch_R1 would
#    loop: R1→R2→R3→R1→... staying in the Right cluster forever.
#    These edges cross ideological boundaries. They let us test whether
#    the graph has any cross-cutting recommendations or is fully siloed.
#    Following the path: ch_L1 → ch_C1 → ch_R1 → ch_R2
#    This chain drifts from Left to Right. The simulator should detect
#    this as rightward ideological drift. This is the core scenario
#    the entire project is designed to measure.
#    ch_island has NO rows in this file — neither as FROM nor as TO.
#    It exists in test_nodes.csv but has zero connections. The code

#    RELEVANT_IMPRESSIONS_DAILY ranges from 10.0 to 200.0. When the
#    be followed more often. The L1→L2 edge (200.0) is much "stronger"
